title: ranunculus pyrenaeus var. plantagineus
intro: Ranunculus pyrenaeus var. plantagineus, also known as the mountain buttercup or plantain buttercup, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus pyrenaeus var. plantagineus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the leaves and flowers, which can be used topically to treat various skin issues.
